title: Euplotes crassus Micronuclear Enrichment by PFGE
author: Rachele Cesaroni
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zwff7bn
materials: CHEF Mapper XA Pulsed Field Electrophoresis

As much as possible of the sea water was removed and cells were resuspended in 1V of 1XTE buffer and mixed with 1V of 2% Certified Low-Melt Agarose (Bio-Rad) in 1XTE buffer, then quickly transferred into the wells of the plug mold to produce the plugs (around 150,000 cells in 80 ul of volume in each well).
The plugs were washed three times in 0.5 M EDTA pH 8.0 for 10 min each, then inserted into the wells of the 1% Certified Low-Melt Agarose gel (Bio-Rad).

The instrument used for the PFGE was CHEF Mapper XA Pulsed Field Electrophoresis System (Bio-Rad). 2.2 L of 0.5X TBE buffer were poured into the electrophoresis chamber for a pre-run of 30 min at 14°C, with a pump speed setting of 70 (flow rate of ~ 1 liter/minute).
When the run was completed the gel was stained with SYBR Safe DNA Gel Stain (Thermo Fisher Scientific) for 60 min and visualized with blue light.

The bands of interest were cut and melted at 65°C for around 30 min. The gel volume was determined and digested with Agarase from Pseudomonas atlantica (Sigma) at 40°C overnight and then with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 10 µg/ml for 2 h at 37°C.

. The DNA was extracted by Phenol/Chloroform, precipitated by 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.2/absolute EtOH, resuspended in 30 ml of ddH2O, and quantified by NanoDropTM 1000 UV-Vis Spectrophotometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific).

steps.title: Starved Euplotes crassus cells (treated overnight...
steps.instruction: Starved Euplotes crassus cells (treated overnight with ampicillin 100 mg/ml) were filtered and harvested by centrifugation at 400 rcf for 3 min.

steps.title: Plugs were set at 4°C for...
steps.instruction: Plugs were set at 4°C for at least 30 min and then placed in 5 ml cells lysis buffer (1% N-Lauroylsarcosine sodium salt, 1% SDS, 1 mg/ml Proteinase K, 0.5 M EDTA pH 8.0) at 60°C overnight.

steps.title: The run was set with a...
steps.instruction: The run was set with a gradient of 6 V/cm, a time of 30 h, an included angle of 120°, an initial switch time of 70 sec, a final switch time of 70 sec and a linear ramping factor.
